column1: I. Ground of Faith (十信, Ten Faiths)
These stages establish the foundation of aspiration and trust in the path.
II. Ground of Dwelling (十住, Ten Dwellings)
The bodhisattva "dwells" in deeper stages of realization and commitment.
III. Ground of Practice (十行, Ten Practices)
Active engagement in bodhisattva conduct.
IV. Ground of Transference (十回向, Ten Transferences)
Dedication of merit and realization for the benefit of all beings.
V. Ground of Development (十地, Ten Bhūmis)
The classic "Ten Grounds" of bodhisattva realization, culminating in Buddhahood61011:
VI. Ground of Ultimate Realization
The final approach to Buddhahood.
Key Features and Symbolism
The 52-stage path integrates both gradual and sudden aspects of enlightenment, reflecting the Huayan and Zen synthesis123.
Stages 1–40: Faith, dwelling, practice, and transferences—like seeds sprouting and plants growing.
Stages 41–52: Ten Bhūmis and ultimate stages—like flowers bearing fruit and full illumination78.
Stage 52 (Miao Jue/Myōgaku): Ultimate awakening, correlating with Zen's satori or "ordinary mind" realization38.
References to the ten bhūmis and their perfections:
Joy (Dāna, generosity)
Purity (Śīla, morality)
Luminous (Kṣānti, patience)
Radiant (Vīrya, vigor)
Difficult to Conquer (Dhyāna, meditation)
Obviously Transcendent (Prajñā, wisdom)
Gone Afar (Upāya, skillful means)
Immovable (Aspiration)
Good Discriminating Wisdom (Power)
Cloud of Dharma (Primordial wisdom)61011
This structure is the standard presentation in the Avataṃsaka Sūtra tradition, widely referenced in East Asian Mahayana Buddhism671011.
